headline: Google might not kneecap the Pixel 11a with an old processor
summary: Leak suggests the Pixel 11a will feature the flagship Tensor G6 processor. This marks a shift from the Pixel 10a, which used the previous-gen Tensor G4. The 'a' series typically used modern chips, making the 10a's G4 a disappointment.

headline: Google is open-sourcing its 3D emoji
summary: Google has made its 3D emoji available for public use in your own creations. The company shared design insights on World Emoji Day. Designing 3D emoji presents unique challenges compared to 2D illustrations.

headline: GoPro’s discounted Max 2 bundle includes $100 worth of accessories
summary: The GoPro Max 2 360-degree camera bundle is currently discounted to $369. This deal includes $100 worth of valuable accessories. It makes high-end 360-degree action camera technology more accessible.

headline: The Guardian’s Carter Sherman fondly remembers being terrified by Ocarina of Time
summary: Veteran reporter Carter Sherman reflects on her formative gaming experience. She recalls being genuinely terrified by The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time. Sherman has a distinguished career covering complex social and political topics.

headline: Call of Duty Movie to Take Place in the Modern Warfare Universe, Director Confirms
summary: The upcoming Call of Duty movie will be set in the popular Modern Warfare universe. Paramount Pictures and Activision officially confirmed this exciting detail. This aligns the film with one of the franchise's most beloved storylines.

headline: Christopher Nolan's Long and Winding Road to The Odyssey
summary: Christopher Nolan's latest epic, 'The Odyssey', draws on his nearly three-decade career. The article explores the filmmaker's journey to this ambitious project. It highlights how his past works inform his current cinematic vision.

headline: Disco Elysium Dev ZA/UM Announces Layoffs Affecting Up to 32 Staff
summary: Disco Elysium developer ZA/UM has announced significant staff layoffs. Up to 32 employees are affected by the job cuts. The layoffs come just two months after their new game, Zero Parades: For Dead Spies, launched.

headline: Christopher Nolan’s Take on the Gods of The Odyssey Is the Key to Unlocking the Film’s Biggest Mystery
summary: Christopher Nolan utilizes gods, like Zendaya's Athena, to frame 'The Odyssey's' central mystery. The concept of 'Zeus' Law' is crucial to understanding the film's narrative. This approach is key to unlocking the movie's biggest secrets.

headline: Andy Burnham to scrap digital ID to focus on cost of living in first major policy pledge
summary: Incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham pledges to scrap digital ID plans. His government will instead prioritize the critical cost of living crisis. This marks his first significant policy announcement as leader.

headline: Two US troops killed and one missing after Iranian attack in Jordan
summary: Two US troops were killed and one is missing after an Iranian attack in Jordan. Hostilities between Iran and the US have re-escalated significantly this week. Tehran has targeted US military facilities across the Gulf region.

headline: Ruthless plotter? Flip-flopper? Dad dancer? Insiders reveal the real Andy Burnham
summary: Laura Kuenssberg investigates the true persona of incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham. Over 20 insiders share their perspectives on the enigmatic politician. The article explores various public perceptions and behind-the-scenes insights.

headline: Burnham is 'people pleaser' with 'airy fairy' plans, says Kemi Badenoch
summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch criticized incoming PM Andy Burnham. She labeled his policy plans as 'airy fairy' and called him a 'people pleaser.' Badenoch accused Burnham of misunderstanding the country's priorities.
